pulse: The Financials sector currently trades at a median EV/EBITDA of 7.8x and a P/E of 14.9x, compared to the S&P 500's forward P/E of 21.6x, suggesting relative undervaluation despite a robust median revenue growth of 10.6%. Notably, Citigroup (C) presents a wide valuation spread with a trailing P/E of 15.8x but a lower profit margin of 18.9%, contrasted with Goldman Sachs (GS) at 16.7x P/E and a stronger revenue growth of 15.2%, highlighting potential mispricing in growth expectations. As the earnings season progresses, investors should favor financials like JPMorgan Chase (JPM) and Bank of America (BAC) with stable margins and modest growth, offering lower P/E ratios in a shifting macro environment where cyclicals may outperform tech.
